_meta.scope: SOLD: inbound DM triage on two platforms. NOT SOLD: newsletter, invoices, outbound.
_meta.note: Fictional example. Single source of truth for this install. ASCII only: these values are rendered into outbound messages. No secrets here — secrets live in context/secrets.local.json (gitignored).

config.INSTALL_HOME: TO_PROVIDE - absolute path on the target machine, or pass it as the CLI argument

context.BOOKING_URL: TO_PROVIDE - the single conversion link; every interested person is routed here
context.OUT_OF_SCOPE_TOPICS: anything outside the sold workstreams; refuse politely, name no third party
context.LEGAL_BASIS: TO_PROVIDE - the lawful basis for processing inbound DM data (e.g. legitimate interest, consent), named by the client, in writing
context.DATA_MINIMISATION: handle, segment, timestamp, booking status - and nothing else: the rest of a DM is read and dropped.
context.DATA_RETENTION: TO_PROVIDE - how long DM/lead data is kept, and the command or job that deletes it at the end of that window
context.STANDARD_REPLY: short answer, one concrete question, then the booking link
context.SENSITIVE_CASES: TO_PROVIDE - what counts as urgent here + who gets pinged, verbatim
context.EXTRA_RED_LINES: TO_PROVIDE - context-specific red lines, on top of the hard-coded ones in the templates
context.PRICING_POLICY: public ranges may be linked; never commit to a figure in DM
context.PROOF_POINTS: TO_PROVIDE - only what is written on a public source. Never invent credentials, volumes or affiliations.
